summary: Governed route and required checks are in place, but Phase 10 still does not satisfy the stronger F1 acceptance bar carried by the package/execution contract, and the remediation introduced contract drift by reframing F1 as a decision-proof without closing or formally re-contracting the underlying readiness blockers.

blockers.title: F1 readiness acceptance bar is still unmet
blockers.why: The truth source still shows unresolved blocker surfaces such as `contracts_freeze`=`partial`, `real_broker_process`=`planned`, `production_readiness`=`not accepted`, and multiple technology claims still `partial`, while the package phase plan for F1 requires every architecture-critical surface implemented and every replaceable surface implemented or ADR-removed.
blockers.remediation: Either close the remaining blocker surfaces with executable proof and updated truth-source state, or obtain an explicit higher-level contract change for F1 before re-submitting acceptance.

blockers.title: Phase-10 contract drift remains between source package, execution contract, and current phase brief
blockers.why: The package phase plan and execution contract still describe F1 as release-readiness proof with the stronger closure bar, but the current phase brief/parent were rewritten to `Decision Proof` semantics that allow an explicit deny package. That leaves operator guidance and acceptance expectations inconsistent.
blockers.remediation: Align the execution contract, package-derived phase contract, and phase artifacts to one approved acceptance model, then regenerate the F1 report, checklist, red-team result, and evidence pack under that single model.
